title: cerastium decalvans subsp. glutinosum
intro: Cerastium decalvans subsp. glutinosum, also known as Sticky Mouse-ear Chickweed, is a subspecies of the Cerastium decalvans plant, a type of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and infections. The health benefits of Cerastium decalvans subsp. glutinosum include its ability to soothe and calm the skin, reduce inflammation and pain, and promote healing. The plant can be prepared as a herbal remedy through infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are used topically to treat skin conditions and wounds.
